channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: What to Know About Iran’s Kharg Island
item.description: The Persian Gulf island, targeted by U.S. strikes, is home to Iran’s main oil export terminal. Any disruption could jolt global energy markets.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Smoke billowing from a flare stack at the Port of Kharg Island oil terminal in Iran in 2017.

item.title: Here is the latest.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Francesca Regalado

item.title: Trump’s War Alliance With Israel Is Reshaping the Middle East. But It Carries Risks.
item.description: President Trump is the first American leader to embrace fighting a full-fledged, joint war with Israel. Washington has tried to avoid that level of coordination in the past.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at Mar-a-Lago in December.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Friday
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Friday would be the most intense day of the U.S. air campaign in Iran as a vital oil supply route remained choked off.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A resident walks through a neighborhood of central Beirut, the capital of Lebanon, on Friday.

item.title: U.S. Attacks Iran’s Kharg Island, a Key Port for Oil Exporting, Trump Says
item.description: The strikes on Kharg Island targeted Iranian military forces, not economic infrastructure on the island, a U.S. military official said.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Farnaz Fassihi and Liam Stack
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Planet Labs PBC, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A satellite image of Kharg Island in Iran last month.

item.title: U.S. Vows to Block Iran’s Attempt to Shut Down Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that the disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz were “something we are dealing with.” And about 2,500 Marines were headed to the Middle East to bolster the war effort.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Luke Broadwater and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Thousands marched amid airstrikes on Quds Day, a government-sponsored event, on Friday in Tehran.

item.title: A New Trump Envoy Stirs Fears of U.S. Meddling In Brazil’s Elections
item.description: Darren Beattie, the U.S. policy adviser on Brazil, planned to visit former President Jair Bolsonaro in prison and meet his son, who is also seeking the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.media:credit: Rebecca Noble for The New York Times
item.media:description: Darren Beattie, who recently became the State Department’s new senior adviser on Brazil policy, was fired from a job in the first Trump administration for appearing at a conference attended by white nationalists.

item.title: Britain warns its citizens in Dubai they could be jailed for sharing photos of damage from airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.media:description: A building in Dubai damaged by debris from an intercepted missile on Friday. The United Arab Emirates prosecutor said last week that taking or sharing videos documenting airstrikes or damage could be illegal.

item.title: More Marines and Warships Being Sent to Middle East, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: Iran’s response to days of aerial bombardment and long-range artillery strikes has proved more resilient than Trump administration officials anticipated.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Navy ships in Nagasaki, Japan, in 2023.

item.title: U.S.-Made Launcher Fired Missiles From Bahrain Toward Iran, Video Shows
item.description: It is unclear from the video alone whether the U.S. or Bahraini military launched the missiles. Iran has frequently accused Persian Gulf countries of allowing their territory to be used as a launchpad for U.S. attacks.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim, Aric Toler and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: To Fight Iran’s Drones, U.S. Taps Ukraine’s Hard-Earned Knowledge
item.description: As Iran has shown, warfare involves far more deadly projectiles than it once did. It’s a problem Ukraine has been dealing with for years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Schwirtz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An American soldier, in Poland, carrying an interceptor drone used in the Merops anti-drone system. The system was deployed in Poland after Russian drones crossed into the country.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz is narrow — and shallow enough to lay minefields.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados, John Ismay and Agnes Chang

item.title: Read Some of John F. Burns’s Reporting From Around the World
item.description: In a 40-year career as an international correspondent for The New York Times, Mr. Burns had a talent for capturing the sweep of history in intricate detail.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.media:description: John F. Burns in 2002 in Afghanistan. Following the attacks of Sept. 11, he coordinated coverage and reported on the war from Kabul and Islamabad, in Pakistan.

item.title: Trump Softens Call for Protesters to Take Over Iran
item.description: President Trump said protesters risk getting shot “right through the head,” a change in tone from his earlier comments that Iranians must seize the chance to take over their government.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump now says Iranians could face threats for protesting against the government, which his war has failed to topple so far.

item.title: Thousands Attend Government Rally in Tehran to Denounce Israel and U.S.
item.description: Several senior Iranian officials showed up at the government-sponsored rally, marking Quds Day, an annual anti-Israel event that was shaken by explosions from the U.S.-Israeli aerial assault.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Demonstrators pumped their fists in defiance despite a blast near the annual Quds Day rally in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: Israel Drops Leaflets on Beirut with Reminders of Gaza War
item.description: Leaflets dropped over the capital referenced Israel’s “success in Gaza” and urged Lebanese citizens to disarm Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rubble in the northern Gaza Strip, in February. Israel heavily bombed Gaza for two years after the Oct. 7, 2023, attack by Hamas.

item.title: Hegseth’s Claim About Killing a Would-Be Assassin Creates a Mystery
item.description: The defense secretary said the leader of an Iranian unit that had planned to assassinate President Trump had been killed. But U.S. officials privately acknowledge the story is not that simple.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Aaron Boxerman and Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth at the White House. “Iran tried to kill President Trump, and President Trump got the last laugh,” he said at a news conference last week.

item.title: How This Oil Supply Shock Compares With the Embargo of 1973
item.description: Governments have stockpiled oil, and cars are more efficient but the supply shock is global, and there’s no sense of when it’ll end.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The 1973 oil embargo led to panic buying, and long lines at gas stations. While that isn’t likely now in the United States, the price shock is likely to reverberate through the economy.

item.title: Hegseth says an officer has been appointed to investigate deadly strike on Iranian school.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.media:description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced the appointment of a general officer to lead an investigation on a deadly strike that hit an elementary school in Iran.

item.title: U.S. Tech Giants Flocked to the Persian Gulf. Now They Are Targets.
item.description: Amazon, Google and others struck deals in the Persian Gulf to foot the bill for A.I. development. Iran has now threatened attacks against the companies’ infrastructure in the region.
item.dc:creator: Adam Satariano and Paul Mozur
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rose after a reported Iranian drone strike on a fuel facility of Bahrain International Airport on Thursday. Iranian drones have also struck Amazon’s data center in Bahrain and two others in the U.A.E.

item.title: Hegseth Vows Lethal Day in Iran as Air War Intensifies
item.description: At a news conference, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth gave no indication of how long it would take before the Navy could escort civilian cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: “With every passing hour, we know, and we know they know, that the military capabilities of their evil regime are crumbling,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said.

item.title: Oil and cargo ships are growing targets in war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Blacki Migliozzi, Christiaan Triebert and Lazaro Gamio

item.title: Turkey Says NATO Defenses Shot Down a Third Iranian Missile
item.description: Turkey did not say where the missile was intercepted. But residents near Incirlik Air Base, which hosts U.S. troops, reported hearing sirens and a loud boom.
item.dc:creator: Nimet Kirac and Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Turkish combat drones at the Incirlik Air Base, in southern Turkey, in 2021.

item.title: What Does It Mean to Have Air Superiority Over Iran?
item.description: The American and Israeli air forces have a dominant advantage in the skies, but Iran can still muster some resistance.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: B-1 bombers are among the aircraft that the U.S. Air Force is using in Iran.

item.title: Phones ‘Ringing Off the Hook’ for Ukraine Defense Firms as Mideast Seeks Help
item.description: Ukraine wants to leverage its defense expertise into security partnerships and to reap potentially vast profits for its arms industry.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer, Maria Varenikova and Brendan Hoffman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Demonstrating the Bullet interceptor drone in the Kyiv region of Ukraine on Wednesday.

item.title: Drone Strike Has Cyprus, and Europe, on Edge
item.description: Allies have rushed to defend the Mediterranean nation, where the drone hit a British base. Some Cypriots wonder why the bases are still there.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: R.A.F. Akrotiri, a British air base in Cyprus, on March 2, after it was struck by a drone that officials said was launched by Hezbollah.

item.title: Hegseth’s Boasts of ‘Maximum’ Engagement Authorities Face Scrutiny After School Is Hit
item.description: The defense secretary has disparaged restrictive rules for opening fire that are aimed at reducing the risk of mistakes and civilian casualties.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: “The dumb, politically correct wars of the past were the opposite of what we’re doing here,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said last week.

item.title: Where Israeli Strikes Are Hitting Beirut
item.description: Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, shows how Israeli airstrikes have affected Lebanon and its capital. Hundreds of thousands of people have fled areas around Beirut and in a huge swath of southern Lebanon after Israel issued evacuation warnings in its conflict with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Christina Thornell, Jon Miller, Joey Sendaydiego and David Guttenfelder

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Richard Pérez-Peña

item.title: This is what happened on March 12.
Surging Energy Costs Put German Industry ‘Really in Danger’
item.description: Four years after the Russian invasion of Ukraine sent energy prices soaring, the war in Iran is posing another challenge to efforts to revive European factories.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson and Andreas Meichsner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Carbon Caps and Emissions Trading Programs

item.title: Trump Removes Sanctions on Russia to Help Oil Flow Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said it was “unfortunate” that the move could benefit Russia, but maintained that it was only for the short term.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Thursday that Russia would not benefit significantly from the sanctions relief but acknowledged that it would see some financial benefit.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: Iran’s new supreme leader delivered a forceful message in his first public statement since succeeding his slain father, as the Israeli military bombarded Tehran and the Lebanese capital with strikes.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Beirut residents watching the aftermath of an Israeli airstrike in the Lebanese capital on Thursday.

item.title: All 6 Crew Members Killed in U.S. Refueling Plane Crash in Iraq, Military Says
item.description: The crash was not caused by hostile or friendly fire, U.S. Central Command said. All six crew members died, it said, bringing the number of U.S. service members killed in the Iran war to at least 13.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Greg Jaffe and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Un avión estadounidense KC-135 sobrevolando Tel Aviv la semana pasada.

item.title: War in Iran Has India Scrambling to Keep Stoves Lit for Cooking
item.description: India relies on huge quantities of cooking gas that is normally shipped from the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Pragati K.B. and Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Workers cooked food using firewood instead of gas outside a restaurant in Bengaluru, India, amid disruptions in the liquid petroleum gas supply.

item.title: A Weakened Iran Hits Back by Strangling the Vital Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The threats to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz are complicating President Trump’s calculations about how and when to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Peter Eavis, Julian E. Barnes and Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An oil tanker anchored in Muscat, Oman, on Thursday. Iran has throttled traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world’s oil was passing before the war began.

item.title: Netanyahu issues veiled threat to new supreme leader of Iran.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel speaking at a news conference at Mar-a-Lago in December.

item.title: Walid Khalidi, Scholar Called Father of Palestinian Studies, Dies at 100
item.description: As a historian and diplomat, he gave intellectual shape to his people and made sure that they played a role in negotiating their future.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Iran Is Laying Mines in the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: A fifth of the world’s oil passes through the strait, making it a critical choke point in global commerce.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tanker anchored in Muscat, Oman, after Iran vowed to close the Strait of Hormuz last week.

item.title: What Is the Strait of Hormuz and Why Is Iran Blocking It?
item.description: With attacks and threats, Tehran is using the world’s most important transit point for oil and gas as leverage against its enemies.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: At least 16 oil tankers, cargo and other commercial ships have been attacked in or around the Persian Gulf since the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran began nearly two weeks ago, a New York Times analysis shows.

item.title: How Do You Feel About Traveling Right Now? We Want to Know.
item.description: With spring and summer travel season beginning amid a war in the Middle East, a partial government shutdown and more, we’d like to hear how your travel plans are changing.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:credit: Scott McIntyre for The New York Times
item.media:description: The war in Iran and headaches from an ongoing partial government shutdown have added a new level of complexity to vacation-planning season.

item.title: New Iranian Leader Vows to Keep Global Oil Gateway Blocked
item.description: Oil prices surged on Thursday after ships came under attack in the Persian Gulf, and Iran’s supreme leader vowed revenge for U.S. and Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Sanam Mahoozi and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An oil tanker burning after being hit by an Iranian strike near Basra, Iraq, late on Wednesday.

item.title: Israel Drops Case Against Soldiers Accused of Abusing Palestinian Detainee
item.description: After a prisoner arrived at a hospital with broken ribs and a torn rectum, Israelis were once again at odds over the issue of mistreatment and impunity.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Sde Teiman base in southern Israel, where former detainees and some Israeli soldiers have said that guards routinely abuse detained Gazans.

item.title: Iraq vents anger at strikes on former militias now under government control.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.media:description: Smoke rising from an attack at an airport in Erbil, Iraq, on Thursday.

item.title: Yanar Mohammed, 65, Iraqi Women’s Rights Advocate, Is Killed by Gunmen
item.description: She established a network of safe houses for abused women and was an outspoken critic of her country’s repressive institutions, despite the constant threat of violence.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:description: Yanar Mohammed in 2003. She co-founded the Organization of Women’s Freedom in Iraq to protect victims of rape, physical abuse and other forms of ill treatment.
